Vault configurationStep 1 · connect Vault. Step 2 · store AWS keys in it. Keys never touch the DB.

Infrastructure

Cloud credentials (Vault), Terraform state, and managed-Kubernetes cluster provisioning (EKS · GKE · AKS).

Cloud credentialsProvider you use to authenticate Terraform runs
Azure · release
Terraform state backendCloud-aware: S3 / GCS / azurerm chosen from project cloud. Set once per env.
Create AKS clusterInteractive wizard (auto-picks EKS/GKE/AKS from project cloud)
AKS wizard — 5 pages: cluster basics → security → node pools → add-ons → networking + repo
Delete orphaned GKE clusterUses stored env creds; no gcloud needed. Typical delete 3-6 min.
Terraform pipelineinit → plan → apply against the env's cloud creds + state backend.
Environment
release ▾

Connection

Connect a running Kubernetes cluster (EKS · AKS · GKE). The kubeconfig is stored encrypted on the environment.

Connected clustersPersist on the environment; the AI chat queries these directly.
  • connected aks-prod · release · 6 nodes ready
  • connected gke-beta · beta · 3 nodes ready
Terraform state backendCloud-aware: Azure blob container for AKS applies.
Connect Kubernetes clusterPick a cloud, point at a running cluster, and connect.

    Standard_D8s_v3 → D4s_v3 · $84/mo

    idle 45 days · $18/mo
  • Tier object storage to Infrequent Access
    7TB in Standard, low access · $85/mo

Knowledge

Runbooks, incident postmortems, and reference docs the agent reads.

allrunbookspostmortemsreference

Runbooks · 3

Rotate cloud credsrelease · 5 min
runbook
Step-by-step for cycling AWS/Azure/GCP keys without downtime.
Recover orphaned GKE clusterrelease · 8 min
runbook
When terraform state loses track of a cluster that got created.
Rebuild AKS after quota bumpbeta · 12 min
runbook
Delete → wait → recreate flow for regional AKS.

Postmortems · 2

2026-07-13 · GKE apply timeoutimpact: 24 min lost
postmortem
Root cause: 25m runner cap. Fix: bumped to 60m, added resource-level timeouts.
2026-07-10 · S3 backend forced onto GCPimpact: 3 failed applies
postmortem
Root cause: runner blindly read tfBackendBucket. Fix: pickBackendForEnv() dispatches by cloud kind.

Autonomous agent runs — scheduled or on-demand. Findings surface as alerts.
